title: In Pursuit of Insight
html:
They are about ten minutes down the road when it occurs to Lucas. "Your parents," he says, and stops.

Lucy keeps walking for one more step before she stops too. She turns partway toward him, not quite facing him.

"I didn't think to ask earlier," he says.

"They're in the city," she says. She is quiet for a moment. "I don't know if they're okay. I thought about it the whole time I was at the construction site, but there's no way to check." She looks at the road ahead of them. "I accepted that I probably can't know. I hope they can see that I'm still alive, wherever they are. But I'm not counting on anything." She says it without breaking apart, which takes something, and she is clearly aware it takes something but would rather not discuss that part.

Lucas stays with it for a moment. He knows the shape of what she's describing — not the specific grief, which is hers, but the logic of it: accepting something you can't verify because the alternative is standing still. He has less experience at comfort than he does at most things.

"I can't tell you it's going to be fine," he says. "But surviving this is the only thing that keeps the question open. So that's what we're doing." He starts walking again.

Lucy follows after a second. She wipes her face once with the back of her wrist and doesn't mention it.

They walk in silence for a few minutes. The road has widened; they are properly inside the city's edge now, the buildings closer together and taller, the street layout more deliberate. Most of the ground-floor windows are intact. The crashed cars get more frequent the further in they go, each one with its own small story in how it ended up at that angle. The road ahead curves slightly and the buildings on both sides press closer.

Then Lucy walks into Lucas's back. He has stopped without warning, right hand up flat behind him. She catches herself and goes still. He turns his head just enough to mouth something and puts his finger to his lips.

She breathes quietly and looks past his shoulder.

The road around the curve has a wide section where an intersection opens up, and the intersection is full of crashed vehicles from all four directions — a full-scale pileup, doors hanging open, a delivery truck canted at a steep angle against a lamp post. Around and between the vehicles, 11 zombies move at a slow drift. Not random. They are moving in a rough circuit, returning to the same zone, some facing outward toward the approaches, one set near the center of the wreckage.

Lucas pulls her gently to the side of the nearest building and they watch from the corner. He leans in and keeps his voice at barely a breath. "There's a leader in that group."

"How do you know?" she whispers.

"Look at the road behind us. Every block since we turned onto this street — empty. Crashed cars, doors open, but no zombies scattered around individually. I've been noticing it." He watches the group in the intersection. "In the village market, on the second day, I was jumped by two zombies from the front while a third one came around behind me. At the time I thought it was coincidence. Later I worked out that the third zombie was a Level 2 — I didn't get the EXP notification until after the fight. Level 2 zombies are smarter. They can direct others." He nods toward the intersection. "Count them. The eleven in the middle — the one near the back, see how the others space around it? Four of them have been cycling through positions around that same zombie since we've been watching. That one doesn't move on its own."

Lucy counts. She watches the pattern. The zombie he means is standing near the bumper of the delivery truck, the others drifting back toward it at intervals, forming and reforming a loose perimeter. She sees it. "So the strong ones lead and the weak ones protect them," she says.

"Same logic as pack animals. Except these were people, so the structure is more deliberate than instinct." He pauses. "If this keeps scaling up, the stronger zombies become the center of larger and larger groups. The whole city could be running on that structure right now, with the highest-level zombies somewhere in the middle and thousands of Level 1s around them."

Lucy stares at the intersection. The image that comes into her head is not a pleasant one and she does not say so out loud. "What do we do about these eleven."

"You're going to shout," he says.

She looks at him.

"The lower-level zombies react to sound before they look for a source," he says. "When they come toward you, use the katana on the ones in reach. Go for the neck — you don't need to take the head all the way off, you just need to sever enough that it stops functioning. I'll take the leader first, then clear the rest. Don't get surrounded and don't let any of them get behind you." He meets her eyes. "Your Vit is 32. That's not nothing. But don't test it."

Lucy grips the katana. Her palms are wet. She nods.

He moves around her without further discussion, steps into the open road at an angle, and throws the spear hard at the leader zombie from thirty meters out. The point catches it in the shoulder and drives it back into the delivery truck with a Bang! against the metal. It stays upright. He pulls his dagger out and runs toward it.

"Hey!" Lucy shouts from the corner. Her voice carries clearly across the intersection. "Over here!"

Eight of the ten lower zombies turn. They move toward her voice, splitting away from the leader, and she steps out from the corner to give them a clear line and swings the katana in a wide two-handed arc before the first one reaches her. The blade catches the lead zombie across the throat with a Squelch! and the zombie drops, still twitching. The second one comes from the left before she finishes the turn and she cuts across its neck with a Crack! of bone, not clean but deep enough. The third zombie comes in at close range while she is still off-balance from the second swing and she drives the blade straight forward into its collarbone and shoves it backward, then brings the pommel down across the back of its skull.

---

[Level 1 Zombie Killed, Awarding 50 Exp!]

[Level 1 Zombie Killed, Awarding 50 Exp!]

[Level 1 Zombie Killed, Awarding 50 Exp!]

[Received 15 Coins!]

[Leveling Exp Reach: 190/100!]

[Leveling Up: Level 3!]

[Receiving 2 Stats Points and 1 Skills Point!]

---

The system notifications stack in her head and her arms give out at the same time. The katana drops from her hands with a clatter that she does not have the strength to prevent. Her legs follow it down and she hits the road surface on one knee, then both, one hand catching herself before her face goes into the asphalt. The arm that swung the katana is burning from elbow to wrist and she cannot make a fist with it. Her vision goes grey at the edges for a few seconds and she puts her forehead down against the back of her hand and breathes.

Across the intersection, Lucas reaches the leader zombie while it is still pinned half against the truck by the spear. He drives the dagger into its left eye with a Squelch! and twists, and the zombie goes slack against the truck frame. He plants his foot on its chin to hold the head still, pulls the dagger free, and wrenches the spear out of its shoulder.

Five zombies remain between him and Lucy. Three are the ones that didn't turn when she shouted, still moving in a loose cluster near the center of the wreck. Two more are the ones she diverted that haven't reached her yet.

He throws the spear at the nearest of the cluster and it punches through the zombie's nose with a Crack!, dropping it straight down. He grabs the door of the nearest crashed car, a smaller sedan that has come to rest against the curb with both front doors swung open, and drives the edge of one door into the two zombies advancing on Lucy, catching them both across the midsection. They go sideways and down, legs tangled together under the door's weight, arms still working.

Two zombies left in the cluster. Lucas retrieves the spear from the dropped zombie's skull and throws it through the nearer one's temple at close range with a Bang! against the skull plate. The last one reaches him before he can get the spear free and he takes a step backward to create distance, draws the dagger, and drives it up through the underside of the zombie's jaw as it leans in, pushing until the tip finds the skull cavity. The zombie goes still standing, held upright briefly by the angle of the blade, then folds sideways as he withdraws it.

He goes back to the two pinned under the car door. They are still trying to get free. He crouches beside the nearer one and pushes the dagger through its eye socket with a Squelch!, then does the same to the second. He stands, looks around the intersection. Nothing moving.

He walks to where Lucy is still on her hands and knees in the road. She has the katana back in one hand but is using it as a brace rather than holding it, the blade flat against the road surface. He crouches beside her.

"Arm?" he asks.

"The whole thing," she says, not raising her head. "I didn't know it would be that heavy."

"Katana's a two-handed weapon for a reason." He sits back on his heels and looks at her stats in his head. Str 12, Agi 7. She swung a 60-centimeter blade three times in under thirty seconds at something that can push back. He is mildly surprised her arm is still attached. "You did fine," he says, which is true and also the most useful thing he can say right now.

Lucy gets her breathing down and lifts her head. The grey has cleared from her vision. She looks at the bodies in the intersection, then at the katana on the road beside her. "I leveled up," she says. She sounds like someone who is not sure yet whether to be satisfied or alarmed about that.

"To Level 3," Lucas says. "You'll want to allocate those stat points before we go further in."
